rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TOPAZ WAY TOWNHOMES II CONDOMINIUM OWNERS'
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Amendment To Article Xvi, Section 1 Of The Declaration Of Establishment Of Covenants, Conditions And Restrictions Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 4275
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 20, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER TOPAZ WAY TOWNHOMES II CONDOMINIUM OWNERS' Notice Of Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Amendment To Article Xvi, Section 1 Of The Declaration Of Establishment Of Covenants, Conditions And Restrictions Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 4275 Hearing Required for the moving party to present a proof of service on lenders or address why lenders have not/should not have been served. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BINGSEN LI VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Order Substituting Plaintiff In Interest And Granting Leave To File A Supplemental Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 20, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF BINGSEN LI Motion For An Order Substituting Plaintiff In Interest And Granting Leave To File A Supplemental Complaint Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Motion and all supporting papers must be filed and served in accordance with CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. No requisite declaration was timely filed. Without any explanation and without leave of Court, a purported declaration in support of this Motion was filed on November 13, 2019 without proof of service. This filing was 5 court days before the hearing. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL E. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend To First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 20, 2019 line 3. CROSS COMPLAINANT FILLMORE DEVELOPMENT COMMERCIAL, LLC, EM JOHNSON INTEREST, INC. MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Amend To First Amended Cross-Complaint GRANTED. First Amended Cross-Complaint to be filed and served within 30 days of this order The Court will not address the sufficiency of the pleadings at this time. Cross-Complainant may amend the proposed First Amended Cross-Complaint to address issues raised in opposition to this motion. When First Amended Cross-Complaint is filed, and Defendant determines that a demurrer is warranted, parties to engage in good faith meet and confer efforts and file detailed declarations to this effect. = (501/CFH)
